response.justification: This is a strong, long-tenured full-stack software engineer with breadth across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/systems work, plus genuine low-latency, high-throughput backend experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. However, the Principal Performance Architect role is hardware-centric: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows none of these. The C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and a C++ acoustics thesis are the closest signals, but they fall far short of silicon/chip-architecture work at the principal IC level. The profile does not meet the core requirements.
strengths: Some systems/C/C++ exposure via National Instruments LabWindows/CVI and embedded tooling
Mature engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference workload optimization or hardware-software co-design background
Career is application/web software, not principal-level hardware performance architecture
